entries.speaker: savanh
lines: Sit. I have questions before I have answers.

entries.speaker: savanh
lines:
The exile. I heard you left the court rather than
stop asking questions they didn't want answered.

entries.speaker: maren
lines: Something like that.

entries.speaker: savanh
lines:
I respect that. Questions are more dangerous than
swords in a place like Valdris.

entries.speaker: savanh
lines:
The Wilds have stayed alive by staying out of other
people's wars. You're asking us to change that. Tell me why.

choice.label: The ley lines affect everyone. Neutrality requires a stable world.
Valdris and the Wilds share a border. If we fall, the Compact comes for you next.
We need your warriors. Valdris can't hold alone.

entries.speaker: savanh
lines:
The Wilds have never needed Valdris. But you're right
— the Wilds need the ley lines, and the ley lines don't care
about borders.

entries.speaker: savanh
lines:
Don't threaten me with the Compact. But... you're not
wrong that they would come.

entries.speaker: savanh
lines:
At least you're honest about what you want. That's
worth something. Not much, but something.

entries.speaker: savanh
lines:
You spoke with Seyth. She told you about the grey
times.

entries.speaker: edren
lines:
She said the world has faced this before. A door opens.
Someone closes it. The cost is always the same.

entries.speaker: savanh
lines:
My grandmother told Seyth that story. And her
grandmother told her. We don't speak of it often. It's not a story
that ends well.

choice.label: This time doesn't have to end the same way.

entries.speaker: caden
lines:
You cleansed the Fenmother. Duskfen owes you. But
alliance is more than debt.

entries.speaker: caden
lines:
The spirits know what the Pendulum is. They've been
screaming about it since it came into the Wilds. The question is
whether Valdris is ready to listen.

choice.label: We're listening. That's why Torren is here.
We need spiritual guidance as much as warriors.
The spirits can scream all they want. We need soldiers.

entries.speaker: caden
lines:
Torren trusts you. That's rare from him. You'll have
Duskfen's voice at the council.

entries.speaker: caden
lines: Then you've learned nothing from the Fenmother's Hollow.

entries.speaker: wynne
lines:
I showed you the haze. You know what's coming. So
tell me — what's the plan?

choice.label: Contain the Pendulum, reinforce the ley wards, prepare for a siege.
We don't have a full plan yet. But we have people willing to fight.
The plan is to survive. Plans can come after.

entries.speaker: wynne
lines:
Specifics. Finally. You're the first person from
Valdris who's told me something I can act on.

entries.speaker: wynne
lines:
Your Forgewright — Lira. Her people damaged the ley
lines. But she understands the systems better than anyone. If she
can help reinforce the wards, Canopy Reach will commit.

entries.speaker: lira
lines:
I can. The extraction damage is reversible with the
right alignment. It's slower than what the Compact does, but it
doesn't break anything.

entries.speaker: savanh
lines:
We've heard the arguments. Now we decide. Each
leader speaks for their people.

entries.speaker: savanh
lines:
Greywood stands with Valdris. Not for Valdris — for
the ley lines that sustain us all.

entries.speaker: savanh
lines:
Greywood will not commit. The risk is too great for
promises alone.

entries.speaker: caden
lines: Duskfen stands. The spirits have spoken. We follow.

entries.speaker: caden
lines:
Duskfen cannot commit. We barely survived the
Fenmother's illness. We have nothing left to give.

entries.speaker: wynne
lines:
Canopy Reach commits its archers and scouts. Show me
the haze stops growing, and we stay.

entries.speaker: wynne
lines: Canopy Reach will watch. That's all I can promise.

entries.speaker: lira
lines: Did we just... win something?

entries.speaker: edren
lines:
We have allies. Whether it's enough depends on what
comes next.

entries.speaker: torren
lines:
Caden trusts us. That's not a small thing. He
doesn't trust easily.

entries.speaker: maren
lines:
Three tribes committed to defending a kingdom that
has never defended them. Remember that when this gets harder.

entries.speaker: lira
lines: One out of three.

entries.speaker: edren
lines: One is more than zero.

entries.speaker: maren
lines: It won't be enough.

entries.speaker: edren
lines: Then we make it enough.

entries.speaker: torren
lines:
They chose what they chose. We can't force the Wilds
to care about a kingdom they've never trusted.

entries.speaker: edren
lines: I know. But I'd hoped—

entries.speaker: lira
lines: Hope later. Plan now.
